summary: Tested forgot-password / reset-password flow end-to-end. Added TestForgotPassword class (10 tests) to backend_test.py. All 54 backend tests pass (10 new + 44 existing). Frontend UI flow verified end-to-end via Playwright: login Forgot link → forgot-password submit → dev link → reset-password with mismatch validation → success redirect → login with new password. Password restored to 'creator123' at the end.

minor.issue: Rate limiter uses request.client.host which behind k8s ingress rotates between 2 pod IPs (e.g. 10.64.135.205 / 10.64.129.146). As a result the 5-per-15min per-(ip,email) rate limit can effectively allow ~10 requests before any identifier hits 5. Externally-verified by sending 7 curl requests and observing all 7 issue tokens (attempts table shows identifiers split across 2 ingress IPs). Recommendation: honour X-Forwarded-For (first hop) or key rate-limit only by email for the forgot-password case.

Default Python urllib User-Agent appears to be blocked by the preview CDN/WAF with 403. requests/curl with browser UA work fine. Low priority (affects only some test tooling), but confusing during debugging.

integration_issues.issue: After /login submit, the app navigates to '/app' (not '/dashboard'). Functionally the dashboard renders correctly, but external tests that expect /dashboard URL path will fail. Consider aligning route naming or providing an alias.

issues: Console warnings: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' from Recharts ResponsiveContainer (Production Status / Created over time). Minor — charts render once container has size, but log noise should be quieted.

action_items: Harden the forgot-password rate limiter to honour X-Forwarded-For (first hop) or to key purely on email — current keying by request.client.host is bypassed by k8s ingress load balancing (verified: 7/7 requests issued tokens).
Align post-login redirect with /dashboard or accept /app as documented route (currently redirects to /app).
Silence Recharts width/height warnings on Dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: routes.py forgot_password: rate-limit identifier uses raw request.client.host which is an ingress pod IP behind k8s, not the real client IP. This weakens the advertised 5-per-15min guarantee. Prefer request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0].strip() or rate-limit on email+UA.
routes.py forgot_password: logger.warning prints the reset URL even in production if DEV_MODE=false — the URL still contains the secret token. Consider gating the log-line behind _dev_mode() as well.
routes.py reset_password: good — normalises naive UTC datetime before expiry comparison. TTL index set on password_reset_tokens.expires_at so expired rows auto-purge.
models.ResetPasswordRequest: token min_length=16 is a nice defensive check; new_password min_length=6 matches frontend validation.

ForgotPasswordPage.jsx: Link target parses dev_reset_url via .replace(/^https?:\/\/[^/]+/, '') which drops origin — correct for same-origin navigation; falls back to '/reset-password' if string is empty.
ResetPasswordPage.jsx: submit button is correctly disabled when mismatch=true; confirm input gets red border when mismatch. Good UX.

success_rate.backend: 100% (54/54 pytest, note rate-limit test passed on this run but is flaky due to ingress IP rotation)

test_credentials: admin@facelessforge.io/admin123, creator@facelessforge.io/creator123 — creator password RESTORED to creator123 after tests.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests clean their own password_reset_attempts rows and use the existing seeded creator.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py now has 54 tests covering: health, auth (incl. forgot/reset), projects CRUD, generation pipeline, exports, analytics, settings, admin RBAC, ownership, share links, and forgot-password. TestForgotPassword fixture uses pymongo to clear password_reset_attempts/tokens before each test and restores creator password to 'creator123' on teardown. The rate-limit assertion is sensitive to k8s ingress pod distribution — may appear flaky in parallel runs; consider re-running serially or marking xfail-strict=False.
rca of the issue: Rate-limit bypass RCA: In /app/backend/app/routes.py::forgot_password, the rate-limit key is computed as f"{request.client.host}:{email}". Behind Kubernetes ingress/nginx, request.client.host is the IP of the ingress pod that proxied the request, not the external client. The preview cluster has 2 ingress pods (10.64.135.205 and 10.64.129.146) and connections are round-robin'd between them, so a real attacker sees up to 2× the advertised limit. Reproduction: clear db.password_reset_attempts for creator, then issue 7 curl POSTs to /api/auth/forgot-password — all 7 return dev_reset_token in DEV_MODE. Mitigation: honour X-Forwarded-For (real client) or rate-limit by email alone (lower limit, eg 3/15m).
